title: Another Name for the Monster Names Angels
display_title: Another Name for the Monster Names Angels
wikitext:
[]: Another Name for the Monster Names (Angels)             Quote Post by BGMLV » Sun Jan 17, 2010 8:28 pm I am sorry but can you really take those names seriously? If you can't call them angels, what would you call them if you had an option? I know they resemble angel names but really what would you call them?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jan 17, 2010 8:29 pm They ARE Angel names .

[]: Re: Another Name for the Monster Names (Angels)             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Jan 17, 2010 8:39 pm BGMLV wrote: I am sorry but can you really take those names seriously? If you can't call them angels, what would you call them if you had an option? I know they resemble angel names but really what would you call them?

[]: Re: Another Name for the Monster Names (Angels)             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jan 17, 2010 9:05 pm shinjidoggysasuka wrote: fallen angels, those names are from a kabbalic texts, names of angels that fell from the grace of god. for short you could call them fallen ones. or the fallen.

[]: Re: Another Name for the Monster Names (Angels)             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Jan 17, 2010 9:29 pm Ornette wrote: shinjidoggysasuka wrote: fallen angels, those names are from a kabbalic texts, names of angels that fell from the grace of god. for short you could call them fallen ones. or the fallen.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun Jan 17, 2010 9:46 pm Ornette wrote: shinjidoggysasuka wrote: fallen angels, those names are from a kabbalic texts, names of angels that fell from the grace of god. for short you could call them fallen ones. or the fallen.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jan 17, 2010 9:57 pm I've seen the book of Enoch mentions for Ariel, Ramiel, and Shamshiel, as spelled in the "Dictionary of Angels by Gustav Davidson". They're not all Nefilim though. Lilith and Adam are obviously exempt, though they're "seeds of life" yet still "Angels". My second statement is for the topic, i.e. "They're not just randomly picking names of Angels and giving them to the "monster" in the show". EDIT: Lilith was mentioned, to what extent I don't know, Pekudei, Breastplate and Efod, Verse 207 . That story, whether "authentic" or not, gets mentioned all the time (I think it was even on the audio commentaries).

[]:             Quote Post by Captain_Morgan » Sun Jan 17, 2010 10:00 pm I was personally hoping that in Rebuild they would do away with the whole "Angel" thing and just go with the Old Ones. We'd get some new designs in there, and have mindfucks at every turn! Cthulhu fhtagn, indeed.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun Jan 17, 2010 10:12 pm Ornette wrote: I've seen the book of Enoch mentions for Ariel, Ramiel, and Shamshiel, as spelled in the "Dictionary of Angels by Gustav Davidson". They're not all Nefilim though.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Jan 17, 2010 10:16 pm Merridian wrote: Ornette wrote: I've seen the book of Enoch mentions for Ariel, Ramiel, and Shamshiel, as spelled in the "Dictionary of Angels by Gustav Davidson". They're not all Nefilim though.

[]: Re: Another Name for the Monster Names (Angels)             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jan 19, 2010 5:45 pm shinjidoggysasuka wrote: look, i know what im talking about

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Thu Feb 04, 2010 6:47 pm Not sure of how exactly relevant this is, but Israfel is the Islamic name given to the angel Raphael in Judeo-Christain belief, the angel of the trumpet and who was a master of music, though Raphael seems to have a completely different role?

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Mon Dec 13, 2010 12:46 am I hate that search tool, difficult to use. So this was the only topic that seemed relevant. So I'm in one of my moods and I'm searching Wikipedia for stuff and I stumble upon what appears to me as the source for Lilith's name . The whole second moon name fits a lot better than Adam's second wife. While the "second moon" did get it's name from Adam's second wife, it would seem that in the case of naming Angels in Evangelion, this would be the source. Slap me if this has been discovered before.

[]: Re: Another Name for the Monster Names (Angels)             Quote Post by skikes » Mon Dec 13, 2010 1:04 am supershinjiasukashipper wrote: look, i know what im talking about... lilith was adams first wife, fallen from grace for sodamy, and beastiality, then made by satan as the mother of all demons.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 13, 2010 1:21 am IrkenEvangelion wrote: The whole second moon name fits a lot better than Adam's second wife. While the "second moon" did get it's name from Adam's second wife, it would seem that in the case of naming Angels in Evangelion, this would be the source.

[]: Re: Another Name for the Monster Names (Angels)             Quote Post by skikes » Mon Dec 13, 2010 1:29 am supershinjiasukashipper wrote: had links about fairys, and unicorns. no joke, they litterally had that.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Mon Dec 13, 2010 1:29 am I guess I don't. I just stumbled upon it and went "Hmmmmmmmmmmm."

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Mon Dec 13, 2010 3:21 am Ramiel (along with many others) is named outright as being one of the angels who lusted after human women--which ultimately led to the birth of the Nephilim.

[]: Re: Another Name for the Monster Names (Angels)             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Mon Dec 13, 2010 3:26 am skikes wrote: If your a Christian, like you claim, you have to believe in unicorns. The bible references them. Quotes from the bible referencing unicorns: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Another Name for the Monster Names (Angels)             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Dec 13, 2010 3:47 am skikes wrote: If your a Christian, like you claim, you have to believe in unicorns. The bible references them.
